info.title: Gen2X API Reference
info.description:
﻿MQTT-based API for controlling Impinj Gen2X features on Zebra fixed RFID readers. 

## Overview

This tutorial provides a walk-through of the steps to use Impinj Gen2X tag features via the MQTT API on Zebra fixed RFID readers, including:

- Protected Mode
- TagFocus
- Tag Quieting
- FastID

### Details

Impinj Gen2X is an enhancement to Gen2's radio and logical layers. Impinj Gen2X tags are required to leverage these features with Gen2X-enabled Zebra RFID Readers.

Users should verify whether their tags support these operations by referring to Impinj Gen2X tag specifications: [impinj.com/Gen2X](http://www.impinj.com/Gen2X)



## Setting it up

For MQTT connection setup, broker details, and topic onboarding, follow the Zebra reference guide:

- [Zebra IoTC MQTT Setup Guide](https://zebradevs.github.io/rfid-ziotc-docs/other_cloud_support/MQTT/index.html)

### Next steps

1. Configure required Gen2X features using the API commands below.
2. Verify settings with `get_gen2x_config`.
3. Stop the radio if running.
4. Start with `start_with_gen2x` and set `applyImpinjGen2X` to `true`.

> NOTE: The radio must be stopped before applying Gen2X configuration. Starting with Gen2X active while the radio is already running will return an error.

tags.name: Tag Protection
tags.description: ﻿Impinj TagProtect prevents unauthorized cloning and reading of tags using a password-based protection mechanism. It renders a tag invisible to an RFID reader unless the correct 32-bit password is provided.

tags.name: FastID
tags.description: ﻿Impinj FastID embeds the tag TID along with the EPC in every inventory response, eliminating the need for separate TID read operations and improving throughput.

tags.name: TagFocus
tags.description: ﻿Impinj TagFocus instructs tags that have already been inventoried to remain unresponsive, enabling the reader to focus on other unread tags. Targets tags in session S1.

tags.name: Tag Quieting
tags.description: ﻿Impinj TagQuieting selectively silences individual tags to reduce tag churn and improve read accuracy in dense tag environments.

tags.name: Configuration
tags.description: ﻿Retrieve the current Gen2X feature configuration from the reader.

tags.name: Control Commands
tags.description: Start and stop the radio while applying Gen2X configuration.

post.description:
﻿Locks a specific tag using Impinj TagProtect so it becomes invisible to all readers that do not have the correct password.

Send the tag's EPC ID and 32-bit access password — the tag stops responding to standard inventory until protection is explicitly removed. The tag becomes RF silent and does not respond to Gen2 commands unless the reader first provides the correct password.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

action.description: Enables Tag Protection on the tag

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

post.description:
﻿Removes the TagProtect lock from a specific tag. Once disabled, the tag becomes visible to all readers again without needing any password.

Send the same tag ID and password that were used when protection was enabled.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

action.description: Disables Tag Protection on the tag

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

post.description:
﻿Grants the reader permission to see protected (hidden) tags during scanning. Provide the correct access password — the reader uses it to unmask protected tags in inventory rounds.

The tag itself does not need to be specified; all protected tags matching the password become visible to the reader.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

action.description: Enables the reader to read protected tags

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

Tested example.description: Enable the reader to see protected tags

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

x-error-codes.cause: Password is not a valid 8-character hex string

post.description: ﻿Revokes the reader's ability to see protected tags. Even if the password was configured previously, the reader will no longer surface protected tags in inventory results after this command.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

Tested example.description: Revoke the reader's ability to see protected tags

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

x-error-codes.cause: Password is not a valid 8-character hex string

post.description:
﻿Protects a specific tag AND restricts it to only respond when physically very close to the antenna (short-range mode).

Useful for precise picking, point-of-sale, or any scenario where you need to ensure only a nearby tag is read. Requires the tag ID, password, and the `enableShortRange` flag set to `true`.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

action.description: Sets the tag to protected mode

tagID.description: Hexadecimal tagID (EPC) of the target tag

enableShortRange.description: true to enable short-range protection mode for higher security

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

Tested example.description: Protect a tag with short-range mode enabled

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

x-error-codes.cause: Password is not a valid 8-character hex string
Target tag EPC not found in inventory
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Verify the tag is in range and the EPC is correct

post.description:
﻿Enables Impinj FastID. Tags return both their EPC and TID (Tag Identifier) in a single inventory response instead of requiring a second read.

This speeds up inventory and eliminates the extra round-trip needed to read the TID separately.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description:
﻿Disables FastID. Tags revert to standard behaviour — only the EPC is returned in each scan response.

The TID must be retrieved in a separate operation if needed.

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description:
﻿Enables Impinj TagFocus. Tags that have already been read stay quiet and stop responding in subsequent inventory rounds.

The reader focuses on finding new or unread tags, which is ideal for dense tag environments where some tags are difficult to detect. Targets tags inventoried in session S1.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description: ﻿Disables TagFocus. All tags respond normally again in every inventory round, regardless of whether they have been read before.

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description:
﻿Silences one or more specific tags by EPC ID. Quieted tags will not respond during inventory rounds while the configuration is active.

Other tags are unaffected. Use `unquiet_tags` to restore them.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

tagIDs.description: List of tag EPCs (hex strings) to quiet

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description: ﻿Restores previously silenced tags back to full inventory participation. Provide the same tag IDs that were quieted — they will resume responding in inventory rounds normally.

tagIDs.description: List of tag EPCs (hex strings) to unquiet

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description:
﻿Reads back the current Gen2X configuration stored on the reader. Use this command to verify which settings (TagProtect, FastID, TagFocus, TagQuieting) are active before starting the radio.

The response payload mirrors the last applied configuration. Returns an empty object if no Gen2X configuration has been set yet.

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

payload.description: Current Gen2X configuration. Only previously configured feature objects are included.

fastID.description: FastID configuration (present only if previously configured)

tagProtect.description: Tag protection configuration (present only if previously configured)

tagFocus.description: TagFocus configuration (present only if previously configured)

tagQuieting.description: Tag quieting configuration (present only if previously configured)

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description:
﻿Applies all staged Gen2X settings and starts the radio. The `applyImpinjGen2X` flag must be set to `true` for the Gen2X configuration to take effect.

**Important:** Stop the radio before calling this command — starting with Gen2X active while the radio is already running will return an error.

**Workflow:**
1. Configure Gen2X features using the set commands
2. Verify configuration using `get_gen2x_config`
3. Stop the radio if running
4. Send this command with `applyImpinjGen2X: true`

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

applyImpinjGen2X.description: true to apply Gen2X config when starting the radio

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

Tested example.description: Start radio with Gen2X features applied

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

x-error-codes.cause: Radio is already running an operation
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Stop the radio first, then retry

post.description:
Stops the radio. The radio must be stopped before applying any Gen2X configuration changes.

Send this command to halt the current radio operation. The response confirms whether the stop was successful.

**Usage:**
1. Send the stop command with an empty payload
2. Wait for the success response
3. Apply Gen2X configuration changes
4. Restart the radio using `start_with_gen2x`

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure
